params.param: The absolute path to the working directory.
The executable of the HISAT2 index command.
The executable of the HISAT2 command.
The executable of the HISAT2 command.
The executable of the HISAT2 command.
The executable of the samtools command.
The executable of the HISAT2 command.
The executable of the htseq-count command.
The HTSeq Docker image
The executable of the featureCounts command.
The featureCounts Docker image
The DElite Docker image
The R SVA Docker image
The R AnnotationDbi Docker image
The executable of the pca command.
The scikit-learn PCA Docker image
The scikit-learn PCA Docker image
The R pathfindR Docker image
The R RCPA Docker image
The executable of the fastqc command.
The fastqc Docker image to use.
The fastqc Docker image to use.
The executable of the qualimap command.
The qualimap Docker image to use.
The Trimmomatic command.
The Trimmomatic Docker image to use.
The reference genome.
The path to the reference GTF file for the analysis.
The directory containing the samples reads (relative to the working directory).
The metadata file name (relative to the samples directory).
The path to the contrasts file provided by the user (relative to the working directory).
The path to the pathfindR gene sets file provided by the user (relative to the working directory).
The path to the RCPA configuration file provided by the user (relative to the working directory).
params.flag: Whether to keep samtools *.sam files after conversion.
params.param: The factor (column in metadata) for batch correction (or "interaction" to use a combination of all of them). If "none", no batch correction is applied.
The software for counting reads, either featurecounts or htseq.
params.flag: Enable the Trimmomatic trimming step before alignment.
params.param: Parameters to pass to Trimmomatic as a space-separated string. Example: ILLUMINACLIP:TruSeq3-PE-2.fa:2:30:10 SLIDINGWINDOW:4:15 MINLEN:36
params.flag: Whether to keep Trimmomatic files (unpaired reads) after moving paired reads.
params.param: Additional arguments for DElite.
The prefix for the DEA files generated by DElite to be used in pathfindR analysis.
params.flag: Skip pathfindR analysis if the output directory already exists.
params.param: Additional arguments for Qualimap (e.g. --java-mem-size=2G).
The name of the DElite folder that must be used in pathway analyses.
The prefix for the DEA files generated by DElite to be used in RCPA analysis.
The database to be used for the pathway analysis. Options: KEGG or GO.
The methods to be used for the pathway analysis. Several methods can be specified as a comma-separated list. Options: fgsea, gsa, ora, ks, wilcox.
The methods to be used for the pathway analysis. Several methods can be specified as a comma-separated list. Options: spia, cepaORA, cepaGSA.
params.flag: Skip RCPA analysis if the output directory already exists.
params.param: The directory where genome indexes should be created.
The name for the genome index.
The file where the merged htseq-count results are stored.
The path to the contrasts file provided by the user (relative to the working directory).
The path to the gene mappings file (relative to the working directory).
The path to the pathfindR tasks file generated by the pipeline (relative to the working directory).
The directory where aligned samples reads should be placed.
The directory where trimmed samples reads should be placed.
The directory where FastQC results should be placed.
The directory where Qualimap results should be placed.
The directory where htseq-count results should be placed.
The directory where feature-counts results should be placed.
The directory where DEA results should be placed.
The directory where PCA results should be placed.
Path of the directory containing the pipeline scripts.
Path of the directory containing the pipeline tasks scripts.

tasks.foreach:
mkdir -p ${working_dir}/${samples_trimmomatic_dir}/samples
mv ${working_dir}/${samples_trimmomatic_dir}/${sample_name}/filtered_1P.fastq.gz ${working_dir}/${samples_trimmomatic_dir}/samples/${sample_name}_1.fastq.gz mv ${working_dir}/${samples_trimmomatic_dir}/${sample_name}/filtered_2P.fastq.gz ${working_dir}/${samples_trimmomatic_dir}/samples/${sample_name}_2.fastq.gz
if [ -v ${trimmomatic_keep_intermediate_files} ]; then rm ${working_dir}/${samples_trimmomatic_dir}/${sample_name}/filtered_1U.fastq.gz rm ${working_dir}/${samples_trimmomatic_dir}/${sample_name}/filtered_2U.fastq.gz fi

if [ ! -v ${enable_trimmomatic} ]; then input_samples_dir="${working_dir}/${samples_trimmomatic_dir}/samples" echo "[alignment] Using samples from ${input_samples_dir} (from Trimmomatic)" else input_samples_dir="${working_dir}/${samples_dir}" echo "[alignment] Using samples from ${input_samples_dir} (not trimmed)" fi
${hisat2} --dta \ -x ${working_dir}/${genome_index_dir}/${genome_index} \ -1 ${input_samples_dir}/${sample_name}_1.fastq.gz \ -2 ${input_samples_dir}/${sample_name}_2.fastq.gz \ -S ${working_dir}/${samples_alignment_dir}/${sample_name}.sam

${htseq_count} --format bam --order pos --mode intersection-strict --stranded reverse --minaqual 1 --type exon --idattr gene_id ${working_dir}/${samples_alignment_dir}/${sample_name}.bam ${working_dir}/${reference_annotation} > ${working_dir}/${samples_htseqcount_dir}/${sample_name}.tsv

reference=$(cat ${working_dir}/${dea_dir}/${contrast_dir}/reference.txt)
docker run --rm -v "${working_dir}/${dea_dir}/${contrast_dir}:/home/DElite" ${delite_image} \ counts_file=counts.tsv \ metadata_file=metadata.tsv \ condition=class \ reference_condition=${reference} \ ${delite_additional_args}

metadata.task-description: Initializes the directories for the pipeline outputs and pulls the Docker images.
Creates the genome index for the reference genome.
Runs fastQC on each pair of input FASTQ files.
Runs Trimmomatic with the specified configuration on the input FASTQ files.
Runs fastQC on each pair of trimmed input FASTQ files.
Moves trimmed FASTQ files into a folder so that they are used for alignment.
Aligns each pair of FASTQ files using HISAT2.
Converts the HISAT2 alignments into BAM format using samtools.
Runs Qualimap on each BAM alignment file produced by samtools.
Runs htseq-count to obtain the raw counts for subsequent analyses.
Runs featureCounts to obtain the raw counts for subsequent analyses.
Sorts the featureCounts output files.
Creates one DEA folder for each specified contrast.
Runs DElite in each DEA folder.
Merges sample counts into a single file.
Performs PCA in the single counts file containing all samples.
Performs batch correction using as input the single counts file.
Performs PCA on the batch-corrected single counts file.
Adds gene names and Entrez Gene IDs to the DElite output files.
Uses the pathfindR configuration file to generate the list of pathfindR tasks.
Runs pathfindR in each DEA folder for each task in the list.
Runs RCPA in each DEA folder.
